keyword.searcher_intent: Decide between these two specific courses. Understand the differences. Find the better fit for their situation.

audience.pain_summary: Considering InburgeringOnline (it's well-known) but wondering if there's something better. Looking for honest comparison before committing.

angle: Honest side-by-side comparison. Acknowledge InburgeringOnline's strengths. Be clear about who each course is actually better for. Win by being helpful, not by trashing competitors.
thesis_sentence: InburgeringOnline and Dutchwithjoost take fundamentally different approaches to inburgering prep — one optimizes for passing the exam quickly through memorization, the other for actually speaking Dutch. Here's how to decide which is right for you.

outline.section: Two Different Philosophies for the Same Goal
outline.notes: Set up the fundamental difference. Both courses help you pass the inburgering exam. InburgeringOnline: memorization-first, optimized for exam pass. Dutchwithjoost: speaking-first, optimized for actually using Dutch. Neither is objectively 'better' — they're built for different goals.
Side-by-side table upfront for scanners: Price, Learning method, Speaking practice, Exam coverage, Flexibility, Best for. Let readers who want quick answer get it immediately.

Be genuinely fair. Strengths: well-established, proven track record, many people have passed using it, structured for exam, video lessons. It works for its purpose — helping people pass the exam through memorization.

Honest critique without being mean. Weaknesses: memorization-based (pass and forget), limited speaking practice, speaking is treated as exam component not learning method. People report still not being able to converse after passing.

Our strengths. Speaking as the learning method. All 5 exam parts covered. Unlimited speaking practice anytime. Self-paced. Built because co-founder was frustrated with memorization courses. €399 flat price.

Be honest about our weaknesses. Requires self-motivation (no peer pressure). No human teacher. Newer with less track record. Not the fastest path if you just want to pass and don't care about speaking.

Clear decision criteria for choosing them. You just want to pass the exam as quickly as possible. Retention/speaking ability after the exam doesn't matter to you. You prefer a well-established option with proven track record.

Clear decision criteria for choosing us. You want to actually speak Dutch after passing, not just have the certificate. You're self-motivated and don't need peer pressure. Speaking practice is important to you. You value flexibility.

outline.section: The Real Question: What's Your Goal?
outline.notes: Help reader clarify their own goal. If the goal is certificate → InburgeringOnline may be faster. If the goal is fluency with certificate as byproduct → Dutchwithjoost. Neither is wrong — they're optimized for different outcomes.

outline.section: Still Unsure? Try Before You Buy
outline.notes: Soft CTA. Dutchwithjoost offers free first lesson to see if the speaking-first approach works for you. No risk to try. Make the decision easy.

value_props_to_weave.pain: Worried about passing the exam but still not being able to speak
value_props_to_weave.prop: Speaking as learning method means you learn to actually use Dutch
value_props_to_weave.proof: Methodology difference — speak throughout the course, not just at the exam

value_props_to_weave.pain: Don't know which course actually works
value_props_to_weave.prop: Built by someone who went through the same frustration with existing courses
value_props_to_weave.proof: Origin story — co-founder built it for her own inburgering journey

value_props_to_weave.pain: Self-paced courses don't have enough speaking practice
value_props_to_weave.prop: Unlimited speaking practice anytime — no scheduling, no hourly rate
value_props_to_weave.proof: Feature comparison — InburgeringOnline limited speaking vs. our unlimited

originality_nuggets: Acknowledges InburgeringOnline's genuine strengths (not a hit piece)
Clear 'choose them if' section — honest about when competitor is better fit
Frames decision around goals (certificate vs fluency) not features

seo.meta_description: Comparing Dutchwithjoost and InburgeringOnline for A2 inburgering exam prep. One focuses on memorization, the other on speaking. Here's how to choose.

aeo_directives.featured_snippet_target: What is the difference between Dutchwithjoost and InburgeringOnline?
aeo_directives.definition_paragraph: Dutchwithjoost and InburgeringOnline both prepare you for the A2 inburgering exam but use different approaches. InburgeringOnline uses memorization-based learning with mock exams and video lessons — effective for passing quickly but users often report forgetting Dutch afterward. Dutchwithjoost uses speaking as the primary learning method — you learn to actually use Dutch through practice, with the exam pass as a byproduct of fluency. InburgeringOnline costs €200-500, Dutchwithjoost costs €399.
faq_questions: Is InburgeringOnline good for the inburgering exam?
What is the difference between Dutchwithjoost and InburgeringOnline?
Which inburgering course has more speaking practice?
Which inburgering course is best for actually learning Dutch?
